sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: Cortex viz binds to a single project root at startup. Users working across multiple projects (3-4 repos) must stop the server and restart it with a different path each time they want to inspect a different codebase. This context-switching friction discourages using the dashboard for quick cross-project checks.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-PS-01 — Switch active project**
As a developer with multiple cortex-indexed projects, I want to switch the active project from a dropdown in the viz header so I can inspect a different codebase without restarting the server.

**US-PS-02 — Register a new project by path**
As a developer, I want to type or paste a project path into the switcher and have it validated and added to the list so I can onboard a new project without editing config files.

**US-PS-03 — Auto-register on startup**
As a developer, I want the project I launch `cortex viz` with to be automatically added to the known projects list so I don't have to manually register every project.

**US-PS-04 — See project names, not just paths**
As a developer, I want the switcher to display human-readable project names (from cortex.toml or directory basename) so I can quickly identify which project is which.

**US-PS-05 — Persist known projects across sessions**
As a developer, I want the list of registered projects to persist so I don't have to re-add them every time I start the server.

**US-PS-06 — Clean view state on switch**
As a developer, I want all views (Docs, Tests, Workflows) to fully reset when I switch projects so I never see stale folder trees, test results, or workflow lists from the previous project.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| Single project bound at startup via `run_server(project_root)` | Done |
| Project name shown in `/api/meta` response | Done |
| Project registry persisted at `~/.cortex/projects.json` | Done |
| Auto-register project on `cortex viz` startup | Done |
| `GET /api/projects` — list registered projects with active flag | Done |
| `POST /api/projects/register` — validate and add new project | Done |
| `POST /api/projects/switch` — hot-swap active project with lock | Done |
| Frontend project switcher dropdown in header | Done |
| Frontend "Add project" input with error feedback | Done |
| Full `_loadProject()` reload on switch (meta + all + graph) | Done |
| Docs/Tests/Workflow `reset()` clears cached state on switch | Done |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
**Folder browser dialog** — Instead of typing a path, offer a file-browser dialog for selecting project roots. This would require either a native file picker (not available in plain browser) or a server-side directory listing endpoint. Low priority since typing/pasting paths works fine.

**Recently-used ordering** — Sort the project list by last-accessed time so the most frequently used projects float to the top. Would require tracking switch timestamps in the registry.

**Per-project UI state preservation** — Cache filter settings, selected node, view mode, and scroll position per project in localStorage so switching back to a project restores the UI state. Currently switching resets everything via a full `/api/all` reload.

**Side-by-side multi-project view** — Show two projects simultaneously for cross-project comparison. This would require namespacing all API endpoints by project and maintaining parallel state in the frontend. Significantly larger scope than the switcher.
